seoTitle: Rocky Point vs Rodds Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rocky Point and Rodds Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rocky Point vs Rodds Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rocky Point and Rodds Bay.
heading: Rocky Point vs Rodds Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rocky Point, Queensland and Rodds Bay, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rocky Point has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Rodds Bay (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rocky Point or Rodds Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rocky Point has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Rodds Bay scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rocky Point scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Rodds Bay scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rocky Point or Rodds Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rocky Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
